query: USMCA joint review July 1 2026 outcome - did US provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6? Latest news on Trump USMCA withdrawal or renewal decision June-July 2026
answer:
## Summary

**Bottom line: No, the United States did NOT provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6.**

At the July 1, 2026 joint review, the US instead **declined to renew/extend** the USMCA in its current form. This is a materially different action:

- **What the US did:** USTR Jamieson Greer stated *"The United States did not agree to renew the USMCA in its current form. As a result, the USMCA is not renewed."* This triggered the **annual joint review process** under Article 34.7, running each year through July 1, 2036.

- **What the US did NOT do:** Invoke Article 34.6 (withdrawal), which would terminate the agreement and requires six months' written notice.

**Key facts:**
- The USMCA **remains fully in force** — tariff-free treatment and protections intact, no automatic termination.
- **Canada and Mexico both supported** the 16-year extension; the US alone objected.
- A 16-year extension can still be triggered at any time by written confirmation of all three heads of government.
- Absent extension, the agreement would expire July 1, 2036 (a "10-year countdown").
- The US announced **bilateral negotiations with Mexico** starting the week of July 20, 2026.

This was a decision *not to extend* (keeping the deal alive under annual reviews), **not a withdrawal notice**. As of July 1–2, 2026 reporting, no Article 34.6 six-month withdrawal notice had been filed.

sources.title: Ambassador Greer Issues Statement on the USMCA Joint Review
sources.snippet: The United States did not agree to renew the USMCA in its current form. As a result, the USMCA is not renewed... remains in force pending resolution of these issues or until the Agreement's termination.

sources.title: USMCA 2026 Joint Review: United States declines to extend Agreement, triggering annual reviews
sources.snippet: Declining to extend was not a withdrawal invocation. Under Article 34.7.4, annual joint reviews are now mandated each year through 2036. Withdrawal (Article 34.6) would terminate the agreement, requiring six months' notice; the US did not pursue this path.

sources.title: U.S. won't renew USMCA, will review trade pact with Canada and Mexico
sources.snippet: The Trump administration decided not to renew its trilateral trade pact with Canada and Mexico, instead opting to conduct annual reviews of the treaty.

sources.title: Trump Administration Decides Against Renewing USMCA, Opts for Annual Review Process
sources.snippet: On July 1, the United States formally declined to renew the USMCA, triggering an annual review process that extends negotiations for up to a decade.

sources.title: Why has Trump refused to renew the USMCA, and what will that mean?
sources.snippet: Trade ambassador says US will not approve trilateral agreement with Canada and Mexico 'in its current form.'

sources.title: USMCA wasn't renewed. What's next for the North American trade deal?
sources.snippet: Most Canadian exports will continue to enter the U.S. tariff-free after the USMCA non-renewal, while certain sectors face uncertainty.

sources.title: US initiates decade-long countdown to exit USMCA trade agreement
sources.snippet: The mandatory joint review set for July 2026 could trigger a 10-year expiration window.

sources.title: USMCA Chapter 34 Final Provisions (Articles 34.6 Withdrawal, 34.7 Review and Term Extension)
sources.snippet: Article 34.6 governs withdrawal with six months' written notice; Article 34.7 governs the joint review and 16-year term extension mechanism.
